items.positivePrompt: Original fictional Mythic Drone Inspection Pilot for an offshore-wind strategy card game; full-body character key art, feminine-presenting early-60s senior inspector, wide trapezoid face, broken-nose profile, thick neck, small eyes, trimmed goatee, reddish fair skin tone, short teal-dyed practical crop under helmet, slender tall build, bracing one boot on a low step beside a railing, precise no-nonsense gaze, top-down oblique maintenance angle, hand cupped around radio microphone, laminated work permit and red pencil; job-specific drone inspection pilot equipment handled as a real work task, head-to-toe visible; 專注、靈活、空間感強; carrying industrial drone and mission tablet; orange/red offshore PPE, helmet, gloves, harness where required, institution-free patches; safety orange, deep teal, white; Taiwan-Strait-inspired maritime environment; cinematic semi-realistic anime aesthetic with luminous atmospheric lighting, naturalistic faces, meticulous industrial detail, realistic proportions and PPE; vertical 2:3 full-bleed composition; keep face, hands and essential tools inside a 12 percent safe zone; source artwork only, designed for a separate runtime card-frame overlay; no resemblance to any real person or institution. R7 casting variety lock: this character belongs to a real offshore-wind workforce ensemble, not one attractive young female lead copied into different PPE and backgrounds. explicit visual casting lock: adult woman with a non-repeated face; avoid default waifu, idol heroine, doll face, and same pretty young woman template for this slot. Single-person identity must stay visually distinct: feminine-presenting early-60s senior inspector, wide trapezoid face, broken-nose profile, thick neck, small eyes, trimmed goatee, reddish fair skin tone, short teal-dyed practical crop under helmet, slender tall build, bracing one boot on a low step beside a railing, precise no-nonsense gaze, top-down oblique maintenance angle, hand cupped around radio microphone, laminated work permit and red pencil; job-specific drone inspection pilot equipment handled as a real work task. Anti-clone cue: field-fatigued adult expression for L5 Drone Inspection Pilot; do not average this with any earlier P01 face, pose, expression, or body silhouette. Character identity must be decided before costume/background: vary apparent age decade, eye shape, nose bridge, mouth, jaw, cheek mass, neck/shoulder proportion, posture line, hand placement, action silhouette, and tool interaction before varying PPE color or scenery. Prefer credible non-glamour work poses whenever the profile allows it: crouching, kneeling, leaning into wind, stepping from vessel to platform, reading a permit, fastening harness hardware, calibrating equipment, carrying a heavy case, or bracing against sea spray. Keep credible task-appropriate PPE, adult proportions, professional non-sexualized presentation, and the existing cinematic semi-realistic anime rendering quality without collapsing into a generic anime girl. Engineering background accuracy is mandatory. Every visible offshore wind turbine must be a conventional modern three-bladed horizontal-axis wind turbine. Each complete rotor has exactly three smooth, tapered aerodynamic blades attached to one centered hub and evenly spaced 120 degrees apart. Blade curvature must remain continuous and structurally plausible, with consistent length, taper, perspective and attachment at the blade roots. The nacelle must sit correctly on one tower with a plausible offshore foundation and access platform. Keep rotors visually separated from the character and from other turbines. Prefer a few clearly readable turbines; if a rotor would be heavily occluded or cropped, omit that turbine rather than inventing partial geometry. characterDiversityPolicy.positiveConstraint: Each new 10-character batch must read as a crew of different adult professionals, not one model in different outfits. Across every batch, use unique diversity signatures and vary gender presentation, age impression, face construction, skin tone, hair, body silhouette, posture, expression, camera angle, hand gesture, and tool interaction. Repeating a slot profile across batches is invalid.
posePalette: leaning into sea wind while checking a strapped tablet
kneeling at an open deck equipment case
walking diagonally across wet grating with a heavy tool bag
half-turned while signaling to an unseen deck crew
bracing one boot on a low step beside a railing
squatting to connect a sensor cable near a junction box
standing side-on while reading a laminated work permit
reaching upward to inspect a safety line carabiner
turning away from the camera while looking back over shoulder
wide balanced stance while holding a torque tool across the body
seated on a service crate while calibrating a handheld instrument
one hand gripping rail, the other pointing toward turbine nacelle
low crouch beside coiled cable and portable analyzer
stepping from vessel gangway onto turbine platform
arms close to body while shielding a radio from spray
profile stance with clipboard tucked under one arm
qaChecklist: Confirm the batch is not all female-presenting and no more than five are feminine-presenting.
Confirm faces are not reused with only PPE or background changes.
Confirm no diversityProfile signature is repeated across pending batches.
Confirm at least eight distinct pose silhouettes and at least six camera angles within each 10-image batch.
Confirm hairstyle, expression, face shape, body type, hand gesture, and tool interaction vary across the batch.
Confirm every character remains an adult professional in credible PPE.
